name: Old Dominion Chocolate Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine the flour and salt.
2. Cut the cold butter into small pieces and mix with the dry ingredients using a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
3. Whisk the egg yolk and 2 tablespoons of cold water together in a separate bowl. Add to the flour mixture and stir until the dough is smooth and forms a ball. You can also process the mixture in a food processor; it will remain soft even with extensive handling.
4. Place the pastry on a baking sheet and bake for 12 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
5. In a saucepan, combine milk and unsweetened chocolate. Heat over low heat, stirring constantly until the mixture is smoothly blended.
6. In another saucepan, whisk together sugar, flour, and salt. Gradually add the warm milk/chocolate mixture to the dry ingredients, stirring continuously until thickened.
7. Add the egg yolks to the saucepan, continuing to stir until combined.
8. Remove from heat and stir in butter and vanilla. Allow the custard to cool for about 15 minutes before pouring it into the prepared pie shell.
9. Refrigerate until ready to serve.
